question_text: Which of the following best explains why we always see the same side of the Moon from Earth?

options.label: A
options.text: The Moon does not rotate at all

options.label: B
options.text: The Moon's rotation period equals its orbital period around Earth

options.label: C
options.text: Earth's gravity stops the Moon from rotating

options.label: D
options.text: The Moon is too far away for us to see it rotate

explanation: The Moon is tidally locked to Earth, meaning it rotates once on its axis in exactly the same time it takes to orbit Earth (about 28 days). This synchronisation means the same hemisphere always faces us.
